rules.description: Email addresses must not be written to application logs.
rules.evidence: Email-like value detected in a structured log property.
rules.risk: Direct identifiers in logs can expose personal data to support, analytics, and retention systems that do not need it.
rules.recommendation: Log a stable internal customer reference, not an email address.
rules.developerAction: Replace customerEmail with customerRef or userId generated by the application.

rules.description: Primary account number-like values must not be written to application logs.
rules.evidence: PAN-like numeric sequence detected in a structured log property.
rules.risk: Payment account numbers in logs increase PCI scope and can create reportable data exposure.
rules.recommendation: Log paymentInstrumentType and cardLast4 only after upstream tokenization.
rules.developerAction: Remove cardNumber from the log and keep only cardLast4 plus non-sensitive payment metadata.

rules.description: Authorization headers, bearer tokens, and access token fields must not be written to application logs.
rules.evidence: Credential-like field name or bearer token value detected.
rules.risk: Bearer tokens in logs can allow account takeover or unauthorized API access until the token expires or is revoked.
rules.recommendation: Log tokenPresent=true or a short one-way hash prefix generated outside the logging path.
rules.developerAction: Remove authorization/accessToken fields and log tokenPresent=true if support needs to know a token existed.

rules.description: Free-form payload fields are not allowed because they bypass field-level governance.
rules.risk: Raw payload logging can silently capture credentials, identifiers, payment data, or regulated content that was never reviewed.
rules.recommendation: Log explicit reviewed fields with stable names and non-sensitive values.
rules.developerAction: Delete debugPayload and add only the specific non-sensitive fields needed for troubleshooting.
